title: Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya
tagline: Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Kakanmaṭh is a ruined 11th century Shiva temple located at Sihoniya in Madhya Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Kakanmaṭh is a ruined 11th century Shiva temple located at Sihoniya in Madhya Pradesh, India. It was built by the Kachchhapaghata ruler Kirttiraja. Only a part of the original temple complex now survives. Some of the sculptures from the site are now located at Gwalior.

topPlaces.name: Sihoniya
topPlaces.description: Sihoniya is a town in Morena district, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. The town is sometimes referred to as Suhania; in medieval times it was called Siṃhapānīya.

topPlaces.name: Bamhora
topPlaces.description: Bamhora is a village in the Bhopal district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is located in the Berasia tehsil.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gwalior Airport (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Gwalior Junction (41 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya?
faq.a: Stays in Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kakanmath temple, Sihoniya?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sihoniya, Bamhora.
